HENRY TIBBETTS

	b.c.1596

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Austin於從大約1596年到1599年出生在英國時，她的父親Richard Austin I52歲，母親Anne41歲。她於1631年之前與Henricus Tibbetts在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。她去世於1677年7月23日之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Austin于从大约1596年到1599年出生在英国时，她的父亲Richard Austin I52岁，母亲Anne41岁。她于1631年之前与Henricus Tibbetts在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1635年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼。她去世于1677年7月23日之后。

nameMeanings.name: Austin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Austin, from Latin Augustinus, a derivative of Augustus (see Augustin ). This was an extremely common personal name in every part of Western Europe during the Middle Ages, owing its popularity chiefly to Saint Augustine of Hippo (354–430), whose influence on Christianity is generally considered to be second only to that of Saint Paul. Various religious orders came to be formed following rules named in his honor, including the ‘Austin canons’, established in the 11th century, and the ‘Austin friars’, a mendicant order dating from the 13th century. The popularity of the personal name in England was further increased by the fact that it was borne by Saint Augustine of Canterbury (died c. 605), an Italian Benedictine monk known as ‘the Apostle of the English’, who brought Christianity to southern England in 597 and founded the see of Canterbury.
English: variant of   Aspden  , with which this surname became confused.

History: This was the name of a merchant family that became established in eastern MA in the 17th century, notably in Charlestown. Richard Austin came from England and landed at Boston in 1638, and his son Anthony was clerk of Suffield, CT, in 1674. The surname is very common in England as well as America; this Richard Austin was only one of a number of bearers who brought it to North America. — In 1821 Stephen F. Austin (1793–1836), born in Austinville VA, founded the first Anglo colony in TX.

variants.name: Augustine
meanings: Americanized form of any of various European surnames from personal names derived from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ), for example German, Dutch, French, and Czech Augustin 1, Dutch Augustijn, Polish Augustyn , Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin (see Augustin 2), Italian Agostino .
History: This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Agostino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Salvatore, Antonio, Nicola, Vincenzo, Carlo, Carmelo, Cono, Cosmo, Domenico, Elio, Enzo, Lino, Armando, Arturo, Atilio, Octavio, Silvana.
Italian: from the personal name Agostino, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Agoston
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Tibor, Attila, Csaba, Gabor, Geza, Istvan, Jeno, Laszlo.
Hungarian (Ágoston): from the personal name Ágoston, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). This surname is also common in Romania.

variants.name: Auston
meanings: English:
variant of   Austin  .
variant of   Owston  .

variants.name: Augenstein
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Manfred.
German: from an altered form of the personal name Augustin (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Fernand, Jacques, Jean Claude, Monique, Renold, Andree, Antoine, Arsene, Celine, Dany.  German Kurt, Fritz, Gunter, Hans, Erwin, Georg, Gerda, Inge, Juergen, Klaus, Matthias, Otto.

French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, and Czech; Slovak (mainly Augustín); Croatian (also Auguštin): from the personal name Augustin, Slovak Augustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of the patronymics and other derivatives of these personal names, such as Slovak Augustiňák. This surname is by far most common in Haiti.

Americanized form of Slovenian Avguštin or Auguštin: from the personal name Avguštin, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened and altered form of the Slovenian patronymics like Avguštinčič. Compare Augustine .

variants.name: Agustin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Conrado, Ernesto, Renato, Rodolfo, Salvador, Antonio, Carlos, Francisco, Manuel, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.
Spanish (Agustín): from the personal name Agustín, from Latin Augustinus (see Austin ).

variants.name: Augustus
meanings: Dutch, German, English, Welsh, West Indian (mainly Trinidad and Tobago), and African (mainly Nigeria): from the Latin personal name Augustus (from the adjective augustus ‘consecrated, majestic, venerable’), or a Latinized (humanistic) form of German and English August . It was the Emperor Augustus (63 BC – AD 14), after whom it became conventional for Roman emperors to adopt Augustus as a title on their accession and in whose honor the month of August was named. The personal name became popular among early Christians, who read into it the implication that the bearer had become greater by being baptized. The surname Augustus is virtually non-existing in Germany.

variants.name: Austen
meanings: English: variant of   Austin  , associated chiefly with southeastern England, especially Kent.
German: from a shortened form of the personal name   Augustin  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Austin was born from about 1596 to 1599, in England, her father, Richard Austin I, was 52 and her mother, Anne, was 41. She married Henricus Tibbetts before 1631, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She immigrated to Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1635. She died after 23 July 1677.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Austin(이)가 대략 1596년 부터 1599년 까지 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Austin I(은)는 52세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1631년 이전 영국에서 Henricus Tibbetts(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1635년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1677년 7월 23일 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Austin nasceu de aproximadamente 1596 a 1599, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Austin I, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Anne, tinha 41 anos. Ela casou-se com Henricus Tibbetts antes 1631, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela imigrou para o Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1635. Ela faleceu após 23 de julho de 1677.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Austin родилась с около 1596 по 1599 в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Austin I было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Anne, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henricus Tibbetts перед 1631 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она иммигрировала в массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1635 году. Она умерла после 23 июля 1677 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Austin nació desde aproximadamente 1596 hasta 1599, en Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Austin I, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Anne, tenía 41 años. Se casó con Henricus Tibbetts antes de 1631, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Inmigró a Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1635. Murió después de 23 de julio de 1677.
